skill.name: azure-messaging-webpubsub-java
skill.description: Build real-time web applications with Azure Web PubSub SDK for Java. Use when implementing WebSocket-based messaging, live updates, chat applications, or server-to-client push notifications.
skill.summary: Use Azure Web PubSub SDK for Java to build real-time messaging, chat, and live update features.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and Java, XML, or shell examples in SKILL.md. The weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md line 3 is not supported by the text, and no prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The remaining low risk is normal network-service documentation for Azure Web PubSub with placeholder endpoint and credential examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are Markdown code fences and language-labeled examples. No executable script, command invocation, or command injection path was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens are fenced documentation examples, not runtime shell execution. SKILL.md contains no tool wrapper or script that would execute those examples automatically.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network Endpoint Examples
low_findings.description: The skill documents Azure Web PubSub endpoint and connection string formats with placeholder values. This is expected for an Azure networking SDK guide, but users should avoid placing real secrets in prompts or committed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and access key values are placeholders, so no credential exposure is present. The skill domain still involves network configuration and secret handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md line 3, but that line only describes WebSocket-based messaging and push notifications. No weak algorithm name or cryptographic implementation was found there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive metadata, not crypto code or an algorithm choice. No evidence supports the high-severity blocker finding.

content.user_title: Build Azure Web PubSub Java Apps
content.value_statement: Real-time Java features require correct Web PubSub client setup, messaging patterns, and token permissions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for Azure Web PubSub SDK usage.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for the Azure Web PubSub Java package.
Explains client creation with connection strings, access keys, and DefaultAzureCredential.
Provides Java patterns for sending messages to all clients, groups, users, and connections.
Covers group membership, connection management, permission checks, and client access tokens.
Includes async client examples and basic Azure SDK error handling patterns.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not execute Java code or provision Azure resources.
Examples use placeholders and require real Azure Web PubSub configuration.
It does not include a complete runnable application or automated tests.
SDK version details may need verification before use in production.

use_cases.title: Add Chat Messaging
use_cases.description: Implement group and user messaging patterns for a Java chat backend using Azure Web PubSub.

use_cases.title: Issue Client Tokens
use_cases.description: Design server-side token generation with user IDs, groups, roles, and expiration settings.

use_cases.title: Review Real-Time Architecture
use_cases.description: Compare broadcast, group, user, and connection-level delivery options for live product updates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to show how to create an Azure Web PubSub Java client with DefaultAzureCredential and a hub named chat.

prompt_templates.title: Send Group Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design Java service methods that send plain text and application messages to a Web PubSub group.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Scoped Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain how a Java backend should generate Web PubSub client access tokens with user IDs, groups, roles, and expiration.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Live Update Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to propose a Java architecture for live notifications with hub isolation, group targeting, async sends, and error handling.

output_examples.input: How should my Java backend send a notification to premium users?
output_examples.output: Use a Web PubSub group for premium users. Add each qualifying user or connection to that group, then send the notification to the group.

output_examples.input: How do I let clients join only approved groups?
output_examples.output: Generate client access tokens on the server. Include the user ID, allowed groups, narrow roles, and a short expiration.

output_examples.input: What should I review before production?
output_examples.output: Verify SDK version, credential handling, token lifetime, role scope, hub boundaries, error handling, and inactive connection cleanup.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential where possible and keep access keys out of prompts and source control.
Grant the smallest Web PubSub roles needed for each client workflow.
Use groups and hub isolation to keep delivery paths clear and manageable.
anti_patterns: Do not embed real connection strings or access keys in examples, tickets, or committed files.
Do not grant broad send or join roles when a group-specific role is enough.
Do not use one hub for unrelated application features with different access rules.

faq.question: Does this skill provision Azure Web PubSub resources?
faq.answer: No. It provides Java SDK guidance and examples. Azure resources must be created separately.

faq.question: Can it help with WebSocket chat backends?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers clients, groups, user messaging, connection messaging, and access tokens.

faq.question: Does it include secure credential guidance?
faq.answer: It shows credential options and mentions token expiration and minimal roles. You still need secure secret storage.

faq.question: Can it generate complete application code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but the skill itself contains focused snippets, not a complete application.

faq.question: Does it cover asynchronous operations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Azure Web PubSub async client examples with success and error callbacks.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.
